name: First Robinson Savings Bank, National Association
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Illinois and Indiana with checking, savings, loans, and investment services across multiple locations.
description_long:
First Robinson Savings Bank, National Association is a community-focused bank operating as a national association member of the FDIC, offering deposit and lending products across Illinois (Robinson, Oblong, Palestine) and Indiana (Vincennes). The bank positions itself as "A Real Community Bank" with emphasis on personalized service and local market knowledge.

The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including free checking accounts (Kasasa Cash Checking with ATM fee refunds), savings products, money market accounts, certificates of deposit, IRAs, and health savings accounts. Loan products span personal loans, auto loans, home equity loans, and primary mortgages. For business customers, they provide business checking, business savings, business loans, term loans, lines of credit, and commercial real estate lending. Additional services include online and mobile banking with mobile deposit, bill pay, trust services, investment services, and merchant card processing.

What distinguishes First Robinson is their emphasis on free account options with high-interest rewards (particularly the Kasasa Cash Checking program), youth savings programs with no service charges, and specialized lending for agriculture. They also highlight drive-thru modernization with self-service options and personal teller assistance. The bank appears to focus on relationship banking and community engagement, publishing a CRA public file and maintaining investor relations.

As a traditional brick-and-mortar community bank, First Robinson is best suited for customers seeking personalized local service and multi-product relationships rather than those prioritizing cutting-edge digital-only banking. Their service area is geographically limited to a small region of Illinois and Indiana, which restricts accessibility for customers outside these areas. The bank's website emphasizes security and fraud protection, suggesting standard industry practices rather than unique differentiators. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Free Kasasa Cash Checking with ATM fee refunds
Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for personal and business
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Personal and auto loans with online payment portal
Home equity loans and primary home mortgage loans
Business checking, savings, and business loans
Commercial real estate and agriculture loans
Online banking and bill pay services
pros: Free Kasasa Cash Checking with nationwide ATM fee refunds and no service charges
FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Youth Savings accounts with high interest rates and no service charges
Multiple loan product options including agriculture loans, home equity, and commercial real estate
Mobile banking with mobile deposit capability for remote account management
Trust and investment services for financial planning and wealth management
Free Kasasa Saver with automatic reward transfers
cons: Limited geographic footprint to Illinois (Robinson, Oblong, Palestine) and Indiana (Vincennes) only
No mention of online account opening or fully digital banking experience on homepage
Limited transparency on specific interest rates or competitive rate information on main website
best_for: Illinois and Indiana residents seeking traditional community banking with personalized service
Small business owners requiring local relationship banking and specialized lending (agriculture, commercial real estate)
Customers prioritizing free checking with ATM fee refunds and high-interest savings rewards
Families seeking youth savings programs with no service charges and educational banking tools
diagnosis: First Robinson Savings Bank is best for residents in Robinson, Oblong, and Palestine (IL) or Vincennes (IN) who value personalized community banking relationships and free checking accounts with rewards. The main limitation is strict geographic service area restriction—customers outside these four towns cannot easily access the bank's services or branches.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_official_name: First Robinson Savings Bank, National Association
